feed.title: SAP Community - SAP BTP Security
feed.subtitle: SAP BTP Security Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: sap BTP BUILD Apps no authorization for ABAP System
entry.content:
Hi Team , 

In BTP cloud i have created destination for ABAP and connection is success and reachable .

The issue is from BUILD App , when trying to access the ABAP system via the destination created in BTP Cockpit we are getting below error .

Error message :  "You don't have permission to access this system. Choose another system or reach out to your admin."

Please suggest solution . 

 

Error Image:

Destination Detail

entry.title: Cloud Transport Management Service -getting error while importing iFlow
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I want to transport an iFlow, but when I tried to import it into the target environment using Cloud Transport Management, the following error occurred, and the transport failed.

Exception during start of deployment for deploy type 'SLP_CTS': Error during client creation: Not Found

Could you please tell me the cause and how to resolve it?

 

The Check Connection results for each Destination (Cloud Transport Management Service, ContentAgentService, ProcessIntegrationRuntime, and the Cloud Foundry API Endpoint of the target environment) are as follows.

 

If there is any missing information, please let me know.

Thanks in advance.

Ryoki

entry.title: Use Destinations During Local Development for Second API
entry.content:
Hello all,

I am currently working on a UI module of a mta project. 

In my application I have two APIs, the default OData service and a REST API and I have maintained instance level destinations in BTP and calling the APIs when the app is running on BTP is totally fine.

I followed this blog and maintained a subaccount level destination as I am using managed app router.

However, when I call the APIs during local development, only the OData is working but the REST API is returning a Http 401 error.

This is my ui5.yaml

 

  customMiddleware:
    - name: fiori-tools-proxy
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        ignoreCertError: false # If set to true, certificate errors will be ignored. E.g. self-signed certificates will be accepted
        ui5:
          path:
            - /resources
            - /test-resources
          url: https://ui5.sap.com
        backend:
          - path: /odata
            url: <odata hostname>.com
          - path: /workflow/rest
            url: <REST API hostname>.com

 

My xs-app.json

 

  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/odata/v2/(.*)$",
      "target": "/odata/v2/$1",
      "destination": "odata_dest",
      "csrfProtection": false
    },  
    {
      "source": "^/workflow/(.*)$",
      "target": "/workflow/$1",
      "destination": "rest_api_dest",
      "csrfProtection": false
    }
  ]

 

The code to call the api

 

wfRequests = await Helper.callRest("/workflow/rest/v1/workflow-instances" + query, "GET");

 

Appreciate if you could provide me some input.

Best Regards,

HB

entry.title: how do we automate space & org members access in SAP BTP cockpit(Cloud foundry env)
entry.content: needs options to automate BTP space & org member access either using some tools or in a different way to reduce manual intervention every time.

entry.title: SAP BAS - BTP deploy failed: throw cds.error `Didn't find auth implementation for ${config}`
entry.content:
Team:

My projects were working at some point. Now, I downloaded from github and cannot be deployed OK. I created a new project and I received this error message: 

 

throw cds.error `Didn't find auth implementation for ${config}`
 
In the past, I created .cdsrc.jos containing:
{
  "requires": {
    "auth": "none"
  }
}
This used to by pass the authorisation checking. Sadly, something has change with security. I modified the MTA.YML and package.json to not use authority checking. The same error message.
 
Any help is welcome.

entry.title: Error "403 Forbidden The request has been blocked by UCON" when creating Handling Unit in S4
entry.content:
Hi,

Context: I have a SAP DM enviroment integreated with S4 Public cloud. 

Objective: Create a HU in S4 from a production process in SAP DM, API: SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Error: When I execute the API in a production process in SAP DM, I get a 403 Forbidden error (see image). Apparently, SAP UCON is blocking the request.

 

 

I tested the same autentification for getting the data from order in S4 and it was working (API:SAP Business Accelerator Hub).

How can I solve this problem? 

 

Thanks for your help! 🙂

entry.title: Separate Client Credentials in a Multitenant REST API Application on BTP from XSUAA Service
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I’m encountering a challenge with our multi‑tenant application deployed on SAP BTP. Let me provide some context and describe the problem in detail:

Current Setup:

  • We have a Spring Boot Java application (not CAP-based) that exposes REST APIs with no frontend UI.

  • The app is made multi‑tenant.

  • For Authentication we use XSUAA service (with tenant-mode: shared) in the provider’s subaccount. This setup allows us to subscribe multiple customers to the application.

  • Currently, for a new customer subscription, the client ID and secret used for OAuth2 are created in the provider’s XSUAA instance. We create a new service key for each consumer and provide that to the customer for use. The customer uses the provided clientid/secret along with their subaccount's token endpoint to retrieve JWT Token and use it for accessing out APIs. 

  • This approach works, but it is not secure enough because the same client credentials (client ID/secret) can be shared among different subscribers. In other words, if Customer A knows Customer B’s subaccount domain, they could potentially use their own credentials together with Customer B’s token URL and access data for Customer B.

Desired Outcome:

  • We want each consumer/subscriber to have their own, separate client ID and secret that are not shareable across tenants.

  • Our initial research indicated that using XSUAA with tenant-mode: external might provide this isolation (see SAP Documentation on Tenant Mode). But it did not work.

  • Alternatively, I also attempted to return XSUAA service details as a dependency during the subscription process (with tenant-mode: external), but that approach has not worked either.

Question:
How should a new consumer/subscriber correctly receive or be provisioned with their own dedicated client ID and secret for accessing our REST APIs? Is tenant-mode: external in XSUAA the recommended approach for this use case, and if so:

  • What are the exact steps or configuration changes needed to enable XSUAA (tenant-mode: external) so that the tokens issued include tenant-specific credentials?

  • Are there best practices or recommended workflows to securely provision unique client credentials per customer in a multi‑tenant scenario where the provider’s app is a pure REST API without a UI?

Any guidance, documentation, or examples you can provide would be greatly appreciated. Thank you for your assistance.

SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment  SAP BTP Security

entry.title: Trust Configuration" in SAP BTP Trial Account – Unable to Proceed with SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

I am using a SAP BTP trial account for learning purposes and, until recently, the "Trust Configuration" option was available under the Security menu. This allowed me to experiment with services such as SAP Build Work Zone – Standard Edition.

However, I’ve recently noticed that the Trust Configuration menu has disappeared, and I am now unable to complete the setup or activation of SAP Build Work Zone, as this service requires identity provider configuration.

Could you please confirm:

  • If this is a permanent change to trial accounts?

  • If there is any alternative way to continue learning or testing SAP Build Work Zone?

  • Whether switching to a Free Tier account would restore access to Trust Configuration?

Any clarification or advice would be greatly appreciated. Thank you very much in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Isidro

entry.title: S/4 content federation to BTP
entry.content:
Hi All,

When i expose/update some S4 roles in the 2nd round, then the roles that were exposed in the 1st run are neither available in Content Provider nor in Site and nor in Role collection. Can you please advise if all roles will need to be exposed in every run.

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: Is it possible to refer the SAP BTP standard roles in xs-security.json?
entry.content:
Is it possible to refer the SAP BTP standard roles in xs-security.json? We have set-up role collections manually referencing the standard role, however we are unable to refer standard role in xs-security.json. Every blog/github code is creating role template and then role collection referencing that collection. However I am trying to refer SAP roles as this will save lot of time. 
Can someone please confirm if referencing SAP standard roles or role collections in xs-security.json is possible or not? If possible can someone share the code snippet how to refer the SAP standard roles or role collections in xs-security.json?

entry.title: Express Node.js Multi-Tenant Backend Application with XSUAA on BTP - Non CAP based
entry.content:
Hi community,

I have created a Multi tenant SAAS application (Non-CAP NodeJS based) and have deployed this app to SAP BTP in the Provider subaccount. Along with the application I have the saas-registry instance and xsuaa instance created in my Provider subaccount. I am also able to subscribe to this application from the Consumer subaccount.


Current Status: So far, I have successfully implemented application subscription from tenant subaccounts using the saas-registry and an xsuaa instance. I’ve also implemented the necessary logic for subscribe and unsubscribe events in the backend.

Note: This is a pure backend application, and I am not using an Approuter, as there is no frontend component involved. The application is intended to be consumed by tenant-specific backend or frontend apps via API calls.

Problems:

  1. When I subscribe app in consumer subaccount, the xsuaa instance (using tenant-mode as "shared in provider subaccount) is not automatically created in consumer subaccount.
  2. To address this, I added event for getting dependencies while subscribing in backend the code is shown in below picture.
  3. Also added "url" for "getDependencies" in "appUrls" and dependencies detail in SaaS configuration file as shown below.
  4. After these changes, I tried subscribing to the app again from the consumer subaccount got an error shown below.

Asking for Suggestions:

  1. How can I achieve the automatic creation of an XSUAA instance in the consumer subaccount when subscribing to the app, to ensure full isolation between consumer subaccounts?
  2. If not then how can I achieve independent OAuth 2.0 Authentication for tenants specific to their subaccount?

entry.title: Cannot unsubscribe after trying to subscribe
entry.content:
Couldn't unsubscribe from Integration Suite.

Try again. If the problem persists, please post a question to SAP Community.

That was my question.

thx, gm

entry.title: Significance of Origin Key in BTP Trust, especially sap.custom
entry.content:
Hi all,

we are about to onboard SAP Digital Manufacturing. With this comes BTP and establishing trust between subaccount and Cloud Integration Services.

That being said, when using the "Establish Trust" wizard, the default value for "Origin Key" will be "sap.custom".

I know about the overall posibility to use multiple Origin Key values to separate different IdPs / trusts within one subaccount, something that is feasible but not the norm, see Using Multiple Identity Providers from the Same Subaccount, however, I'm unsure about the significance of the wizard's default value "sap.custom"

My questions

  1. Is there some kind of built in "fallback" or hard wired lookup for "sap.custom"? In other words, would it be a bad idea to not have at least one entry with "sap.custom" (providing we are going the route of using Cloud Identity Services and not the default IdP)
  2. For our use case we have SAP Digital Manufacturing deployed in the subaccount and nothing more. Am I right that in this case it might be just fine to stay with the "sap.custom" moniker or is it (when planning ahead) a good idea to apply a more meaningful naming convention? And if so, what should I name the darn thing then? cis_dev (for cloud identity services development environment / tier) or something else?

Many thanks and cheers

J

entry.title: How to Export a role collection in SAP BTP?
entry.content:
Can we export role collections in BTP security?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: SAP BTP - Oauth tab/section is missing under security tab?
entry.content: Oauth section under security Tab in SAP BTP is missing. Have all authorizations but don't see Oauth section.

entry.title: Role Collection Assignment to SAP BTP Platform User via Groups in SAP Cloud Identity is not working
entry.content:
Role Collection Assignment to SAP BTP Platform User via Groups in SAP Cloud Identity Service is not working.

 

1). Create Trust between SAP Cloud Identity Service and Global Account in SAP BTP.

2) Create Users in SAP Cloud Identity Service Admin Console.

3) Created user in SAP BTP Global account with email identifier same as point-2.Assign Role in BTP Platform.

3) User is able to log in to SAP BTP Platform Global Account.

>>>>>>>>>>>>> IDENTITY AUTHENTICATION IS WORKING<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<

 

1) CREATED GROUP IN SAP Cloud Identity Service Admin Console with group type AUTHORISATION and enabled Central Store-Based Provisioning in Application Setting for SAP BTP Application.

2) Mapped above created group to role collection in Global Account in SAP BTP Platform.

3) Assigned user group to user in SAP Cloud Identity Service Admin Console.

 

Logged in Logged out multiple times, in different browser but role assigned via group is not effective.

entry.title: CF Deployment failed with DCL files deployment in Cloud Identity Service instance BTP
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I was having issues deploying my CAP application along with DCL files for achieving RBAC & ABAC (Authorization checks) to BTP, running into a deploy issue:

So I started from scratch with a simple application: 

- Install a fresh Trial Account

- Create a HANA DB (mapping to CF dev space)

- Create a simple CAP application in BAS, single table, simple service to display table entries

- Add hana (for production), Add mta

- Create MTA file

- Deploy to CF

I referred the below url’s for adding & deploying  DCL files with AMS authoization checks(RBAC & ABAC).

 

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/cap-java-implementation-for-authorization-...
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/authorization-management-service-in-sap-cl...

 

All vanilla steps, explained in many blogs and CAPIRE. Nothing special, no manipulation of any package.json or any other files. 

However, no matter what I do. I always run into a CF deploy issue  :

I am trying to explore the concept of AMS(Authoriztion Management System) with RBAC& ABAC. I have created the CAPM application in SAP BTP by using BAS(Business Application Studio). Also, I have configured the deployment properties in mta.yaml file . The application is deployed with Cloud Identity Service instance in CF(Cloud Foundry ) environment with out any issues. But, DCL files are not deployed and getting the errors . I tried out in different ways and failed. Added the logs below for your reference:

 
#2025 04 09 12:08:40.398#Z#DEBUG#c49d6141-153a-11f0-9569-eeee0a831419.btp-cap-java-cloud-identity-demo-main-ams-policies-deployer#flowable-async-job-executor-thread-134#
[PollStageAppStatusExecution] 46d8770d-e2ca-45c8-957e-2546dfdd5277 [2025-04-09T11:58:35.168784] Invoking pre-start scripts. (STDOUT, APP/TASK/deploy-dcl)#
#2025 04 09 12:08:40.398#Z#DEBUG#c49d6141-153a-11f0-9569-eeee0a831419.btp-cap-java-cloud-identity-demo-main-ams-policies-deployer#flowable-async-job-executor-thread-134#
[PollStageAppStatusExecution] 46d8770d-e2ca-45c8-957e-2546dfdd5277 [2025-04-09T11:58:35.318862] Invoking start command. (STDOUT, APP/TASK/deploy-dcl)#
#2025 04 09 12:08:40.398#Z#DEBUG#c49d6141-153a-11f0-9569-eeee0a831419.btp-cap-java-cloud-identity-demo-main-ams-policies-deployer#flowable-async-job-executor-thread-134#
[PollStageAppStatusExecution] 46d8770d-e2ca-45c8-957e-2546dfdd5277 [2025-04-09T11:58:35.632962] npm error code ENOENT (STDERR, APP/TASK/deploy-dcl)#
#2025 04 09 12:08:40.398#Z#DEBUG#c49d6141-153a-11f0-9569-eeee0a831419.btp-cap-java-cloud-identity-demo-main-ams-policies-deployer#flowable-async-job-executor-thread-134#
[PollStageAppStatusExecution] 46d8770d-e2ca-45c8-957e-2546dfdd5277 [2025-04-09T11:58:35.633116] npm error syscall open (STDERR, APP/TASK/deploy-dcl)#
#2025 04 09 12:08:40.398#Z#DEBUG#c49d6141-153a-11f0-9569-eeee0a831419.btp-cap-java-cloud-identity-demo-main-ams-policies-deployer#flowable-async-job-executor-thread-134#
[PollStageAppStatusExecution] 46d8770d-e2ca-45c8-957e-2546dfdd5277 [2025-04-09T11:58:35.633302] npm error path /home/vcap/app/package.json (STDERR, APP/TASK/deploy-dcl)#
#2025 04 09 12:08:40.398#Z#DEBUG#c49d6141-153a-11f0-9569-eeee0a831419.btp-cap-java-cloud-identity-demo-main-ams-policies-deployer#flowable-async-job-executor-thread-134#
[PollStageAppStatusExecution] 46d8770d-e2ca-45c8-957e-2546dfdd5277 [2025-04-09T11:58:35.633689] npm error errno -2 (STDERR, APP/TASK/deploy-dcl)#
#2025 04 09 12:08:40.398#Z#DEBUG#c49d6141-153a-11f0-9569-eeee0a831419.btp-cap-java-cloud-identity-demo-main-ams-policies-deployer#flowable-async-job-executor-thread-134#
[PollStageAppStatusExecution] 46d8770d-e2ca-45c8-957e-2546dfdd5277 [2025-04-09T11:58:35.633751] npm error enoent Could not read package.json: Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '/home/vcap/app/package.json' (STDERR, APP/TASK/deploy-dcl)#
#2025 04 09 12:08:40.398#Z#DEBUG#c49d6141-153a-11f0-9569-eeee0a831419.btp-cap-java-cloud-identity-demo-main-ams-policies-deployer#flowable-async-job-executor-thread-134#
[PollStageAppStatusExecution] 46d8770d-e2ca-45c8-957e-2546dfdd5277 [2025-04-09T11:58:35.633810] npm error enoent This is related to npm not being able to find a file. (STDERR, APP/TASK/deploy-dcl)#
#2025 04 09 12:08:40.398#Z#DEBUG#c49d6141-153a-11f0-9569-eeee0a831419.btp-cap-java-cloud-identity-demo-main-ams-policies-deployer#flowable-async-job-executor-thread-134#

I can't find any valuable information on this error, other than some non SAP related blogs mentioning that the stacks are not available on CF. As I did a clean install on a Trial account I assume the BTP environment is installed correctly. 

Would be great if somebody could provide some hints as I'm not able to deploy any application with DCL files  to BTP.

Regards,

Umasankarseelan

entry.title: Connection to "TMS-QA" established. Response returned: "400: Bad request"
entry.content:
ARCHITECTURE:

firoi app deployed in dev space in one subaccount

WHAT AM I DOING? 

Configuring CTMS for BTP for this fiori app, created another subaccount --> subscribed CTMS there too 

now while creating destination from this newly created subaccount to the dev space of another subaccount where fiori app is deployed -- getting below error while doing connection test

Connection to "TMS-QA" established. Response returned: "400: Bad request"

I have already cross verified credentials and checked content on internet, but it is not helpful

if anyone created this type of destination (from one subaccount to one of the space of another subaccount), pls help

#sapbtp @connectivity #destination #sapdestination #fiori

entry.title: SAP BTP Learning
entry.content:
Hello, I'm not new to the Community but I have an interest in building a Learning Journey for myself to prepare for "SAP Certification Associate: Positioning SAP Business Technology Platform As Part of Business Suite." I need to know where to start in my journey to gaining knowledge (training) to prepare for the exam within the next 3-4 months. Finally, I'm not technical but functional;-). 

Thank you in advance, Tina

entry.title: Error in SAP Mobile Services and Cloud Connector without main propagation
entry.content:
Hi community,

I'm facing a persistent issue with SAP Mobile Services and SAP Cloud Connector.

📌 Scenario:

  • I have a mobile application configured in SAP Mobile Services.

  • The destination points to an on-premise system via Cloud Connector.

  • Destination configuration:

    • Type: Cloud Connector

    • Authentication: Basic Authentication

    • User propagation: Disabled

  • In the mobile app:

    • Security is configured as OAuth (via XSUAA)

❗ Problem:

Even though authentication is set to Basic and principal propagation is disabled, Cloud Connector keeps logging the following warning repeatedly:

The Cloud Connector has not been configured for principal propagation. Please configure it or discontinue sending the SSO token in the request.

This happens every time the mobile app calls the destination.

❓ Question:

How can I prevent SAP Mobile Services from sending the SSO (Bearer) token when using a destination with Basic Authentication?
Is there any way to force SAP Mobile Services not to send the OAuth token, even if the app is secured with OAuth?

Any help or experience from someone who has solved this in a similar scenario would be greatly appreciated.

Destination: 

Cloud connector:

 

Logs:

2025-06-11 08:47:03,615 -0500#WARN#com.sap.core.connectivity.protocol.http.handlers.HttpNoAuthenticationHandler#tunnel-client-804-7#0x79d8e706#The Cloud Connector has not been configured for principal propagation. Please configure it or discontinue sending the SSO token in the request.|
2025-06-11 08:47:03,944 -0500#WARN#com.sap.core.connectivity.protocol.http.handlers.HttpNoAuthenticationHandler#tunnel-client-804-7#0xf425f394#The Cloud Connector has not been configured for principal propagation. Please configure it or discontinue sending the SSO token in the request.|
2025-06-11 08:47:04,459 -0500#WARN#com.sap.core.connectivity.protocol.http.handlers.HttpNoAuthenticationHandler#tunnel-client-804-7#0xc45ba802#The Cloud Connector has not been configured for principal propagation. Please configure it or discontinue sending the SSO token in the request.|
2025-06-11 08:47:10,583 -0500#WARN#com.sap.core.connectivity.protocol.http.handlers.HttpNoAuthenticationHandler#tunnel-client-804-7#0x043ba202#The Cloud Connector has not been configured for principal propagation. Please configure it or discontinue sending the SSO token in the request.|
2025-06-11 08:47:13,150 -0500#WARN#com.sap.core.connectivity.protocol.http.handlers.HttpNoAuthenticationHandler#tunnel-client-804-7#0x836b866c#The Cloud Connector has not been configured for principal propagation. Please configure it or discontinue sending the SSO token in the request.|
2025-06-11 08:47:14,072 -0500#WARN#com.sap.core.connectivity.protocol.http.handlers.HttpNoAuthenticationHandler#tunnel-client-804-7#0x62a66969#The Cloud Connector has not been configured for principal propagation. Please configure it or discontinue sending the SSO token in the request.|
2025-06-11 08:47:21,714 -0500#WARN#com.sap.core.connectivity.protocol.http.handlers.HttpNoAuthenticationHandler#tunnel-client-804-7#0x09a22876#The Cloud Connector has not been configured for principal propagation. Please configure it or discontinue sending the SSO token in the request.|
2025-06-11 08:47:21,933 -0500#WARN#com.sap.core.connectivity.protocol.http.handlers.HttpNoAuthenticationHandler#tunnel-client-804-7#0x26fd940c#The Cloud Connector has not been configured for principal propagation. Please configure it or discontinue sending the SSO token in the request.|
2025-06-11 08:47:23,808 -0500#WARN#com.sap.core.connectivity.protocol.http.handlers.HttpNoAuthenticationHandler#tunnel-client-804-7#0x58380bba#The Cloud Connector has not been configured for principal propagation. Please configure it or discontinue sending the SSO token in the request.|

 

Thanks in advance!
